title: Anion Gap Blood Test: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
cks for an electrolyte imbalance in your blood. To do the measurement, an anion gap test uses the results of an electrolyte panel Â or other blood tests that measure your electrolytes.Â 
Electrolytes are minerals that have an electrical charge when they are dissolved in water or body fluids. You get them from the foods you eat and the fluids you drink. You have electrolytes in your blood, urine, tissues, and other body fluids. They help control the acid-base balance of your blood. A few of your body's electrolytes that are often measured in your blood include sodium , chloride , potassium , and bicarbonate.Â 
Some electrolytes have a positive electric charge. Others have a negative electric charge. The anion gap measures the differenceâor gapâbetween the negatively charged and positively charged electrolytes in your blood. If the anion gap is too high, your blood is more acidic than normal. If the anion gap is too low, your blood isn't acidic enough. Both high and low results may be signs of a serious disorder that needs attention.
Other names: Serum anion gap
What is it used for? 
The anion gap blood test shows whether your electrolytes are out of balance or if your blood is too acidic or not acidic enough. Too much acid in the blood is called acidosis . Too little acid in your blood is called alkalosis . Both conditions can be serious.Â
